body_txt: Gaining another's approval is generally agreed to be a complex affair; after all, what might charm one person could easily repel another. The most extreme version of this is where flat-out attacking another person leads to them liking you more, despite all common sense saying that it should produce the opposite effect. Exactly why said violence ends up enamouring them can come down to multiple reasons. In lighter depictions, it will usually be presented along the lines of them initially disliking the individual because they believe they are a coward and thus approve of seeing evidence that they are willing to stand up for themselves. Alternatively, it might not be so much about the violence itself, as it simply being the point that the underlying tension is finally released and allows them to move forward. Or it might be a case that they just really enjoyed the scrap , and thus are pleased to find a Worthy Opponent . However, darker depictions will normally use this to give an insight into the mindset of the individual and use it to establish just how twisted they are. Usually expect the character in question to be left uncomfortable by this revelation. Whatever the case, it's quite common for one party to deliberately goad them beforehand, just to see how they react. Depending on how it's played, it may result in a Bully Turned Buddy or Friendly Rivalry scenario. May overlap with Actually Pretty Impressive and Audacity Earns Approval . Compare Defeat Means Friendship and Defeat Means Respect , where it's specifically winning the fight that brings about respect, Combat Compliment , where it's an individual's skill at fighting their opponent respects them for rather than just being willing to fight, and Commended for Pushback when it's standing up for one's self towards an authority figure that gains the respect. See also Love at First Punch , where a person falls in love as a result of fighting rather than just respecting them, but the two can overlap.
